text:
Once upon a time in a small village nestled among lush green fields, there lived a shy young bird named Oliva. She loved listening to the old owl's tales at dusk. One autumn afternoon, as leaves painted the world in hues of orange and brown, Oliva heard the owl speak of a magnificent eagle who could fly anywhere.

Eager to go on an adventure akin to the eagle's dreams, Oliva decided. She didn't just want to fly; she wanted to find the eagle. So, early the next spring, she took off. Each day brought her closer to unknown places.

Her wings grew stronger with each flight. She sailed westward across lakes that glimmered like stars. Then northward, where fields grew as tall as giant oaks. Finally, southward into vast skies filled with suns.

Days turned into weeks, and Oliva grew warmer than she'd ever been. She discovered places she'd never noticed — birds singing in corners of skies she hadn't
